articles.title: Crypto users are choosing juicy yields over protection, putting billions at risk of hacks
articles.description: DeFi insurance protocols debuted with huge ambitions during the 2020 crypto boom. But as hacks evolved and users chased yields over protection, most of the sector collapsed under the same risks it was built to cover.

articles.title: Users turn to jailbreaking their older Kindles as Amazon ends support
articles.description: It may be possible to jailbreak an older, end-of-support Kindle and continue adding books to it. But doing so carries risks.

articles.title: The $293 million KelpDAO hack shows why DeFi is finally being forced to grow up
articles.description: For protocol founders and security researchers, the incident reinforced a broader shift underway across crypto: DeFi is no longer primarily battling coding bugs. It’s battling complexity.

articles.title: XRP beat bitcoin gains as CLARITY Act advanced, but a real bullrun still needs Congress
articles.description: The token jumped 5% after a Senate committee moved the market-structure bill forward, reviving hopes that legal clarity can pull deeper institutional money into XRP products.

articles.title: THORChain confirms $10M exploit, rolls out recovery portal for affected users
articles.description: THORChain has launched a recovery portal following a $10 million exploit, allowing affected users across four chains to revoke malicious approvals and claim refunds.

articles.title: Sharplink CEO points out 3 catalysts for Ethereum's price to surge higher
articles.description: The rest of the world is “really closely” watching the US CLARITY Act as the nation moves further away from the previous “hostile stance,” according to Sharplink’s chief.

articles.title: Spot Bitcoin ETFs bleed $1B in a week, snapping six-week inflow run
articles.description: Spot Bitcoin ETFs shed $1 billion in a single week as capital rotated toward AI stocks and macro uncertainty weighed on sentiment, ending a six-week run that had pulled in $3.4 billion.

articles.title: Crypto longs lose $500 million as bitcoin slides to $78,000, SOL and XRP down 5%
articles.description: A long-skewed liquidation cascade flushed leverage across the major tokens overnight, with the move tracking a global bond selloff and the worst session for U.S. stocks since March.

articles.title: Bhutan ‘doesn’t recall’ selling any bitcoin, disputing widely-tracked $1 billion BTC drawdown
articles.description: Arkham Intelligence data shows that over $1 billion in bitcoin has left wallets attributed to Bhutan in the past year, flowing to exchanges and trading firms. The country says it has not sold any.

articles.title: US CLARITY Act brings ‘major spike of euphoria’ to Bitcoin: Santiment
articles.description: Santiment warned that the crypto market “typically” moves against crowd expectations, pointing to a recent rise in bullish sentiment driven by renewed momentum around the US CLARITY Act.
